'''Ewan Dowes''' (born 4 March 1981) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. Dowes' usual position was {{rlp|pr}}. He was a member of the Hull team that won the [[2005 Challenge Cup]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Dowes was sport's first owner of a dual rugby code contract.<ref name="Leeds crack rugby codes" />

==Career==
===Rugby union===
Born in [[Carlisle]], Dowes attended [[Sedbergh School]] near [[Kendal]], where he played on both the 1st XV and 1st XI. In his final year at Sedbergh he trained and played alongside future [[rugby union]] international [[James Simpson-Daniel]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Ultimate Challenge for public schoolboy |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/aug/23/rugbyleague.hullfcrl |website=The Guardian |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=23 August 2005}}</ref> A review of his performance in the school magazine states: "Ewan Dowes was well to the fore in all departments, carrying the ball powerfully, scrummaging purposefully." In 2000, he was selected by England for the rugby union Junior World Championships.<ref>{{cite web |title=England side to play Australia in Junior World Cup |url=https://www.espn.co.uk/rugby/story/_/id/15344563/england-side-play-australia-junior-world-cup |website=ESPN |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=13 April 2000}}</ref>

===Rugby league===
[[File:Ewan Dowes Hull FC.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Ewan Dowes in action for Hull]]
Dowes became the first known player to sign a dual rugby code contract, playing rugby union for [[Leeds Tykes]] and [[rugby league]] for [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name="Leeds crack rugby codes">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1305776.stm|title=Leeds crack rugby codes|publisher=BBC|date=2001-05-01|access-date=2007-06-27}}</ref> Dowes later decided to focus solely on his rugby league career, but with only limited first team opportunities at the Rhinos, he joined [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] on loan in May 2003,<ref>{{cite web |title=Dowes to help injury-hit Hull |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/dowes-to-help-injuryhit-hull-105386.html |website=The Independent |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=20 May 2003}}</ref> before making the move permanent a month later.<ref name="Dowes signs for Hull">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/3109933.stm|title=Dowes signs for Hull|publisher=BBC|date=2003-07-30|access-date=2007-06-27}}</ref>

Dowes played for Hull at {{rlp|pr}} in their [[2005 Challenge Cup]] victory against the [[Leeds Rhinos]], signing a new contract at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Dowes pleased to stay with Hull |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/4471456.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=25 November 2005}}</ref>  Hull also reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and Dowes played as a {{rlp|pr}} in his side's 4-26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|newspaper=[[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref><ref name="Dowes praises Hull's self-belief">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/5416052.stm|title=Dowes praises Hull's self-belief|publisher=BBC|date=2006-10-07|access-date=2007-06-27}}</ref> In October 2010, he signed a new one-year contract with the club.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ewan Dowes signs new one-year deal at Hull FC |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/9105107.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=19 October 2010}}</ref> He was released after his contract expired at the end of the 2011 [[Super League]] season.

After spending a year in Australia, Dowes returned to England and joined [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Workington Town sign ex-England prop Ewan Dowes |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19981364 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=17 October 2012}}</ref> In 2014, he joined [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Dowes makes Dewsbury move |url=https://www.eurosport.com/rugby-league/dowes-makes-dewsbury-move_sto4170549/story.shtml |website=Eurosport |access-date=4 September 2023 |date=12 March 2014}}</ref>

He played at international level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], making seven appearances between 2004 and 2006.

After retiring from rugby, Dowes worked as a financial adviser.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ewan Dowes: From rugby league star to SJP financial adviser |url=https://portfolio-adviser.com/ewan-dowes-from-rugby-league-star-to-sjp-financial-adviser/ |website=Portfolio Adviser |date=28 October 2020 |access-date=4 September 2023}}</ref>
